label: FAIL
rationale: ProgrammaticResetEnable was True, so the successful TPER_RESET was enabled. Because LockOnReset [0,3] contains Programmatic, Opal requires ReadLocked and WriteLocked to be set True; returning False/False is not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: Core defines an empty LockOnReset set as causing no ReadLocked or WriteLocked change on any reset. The prior state was locked True/True, so after the power cycle and new session the target Get should still return True/True.

label: FAIL
rationale: The successful Set left LockOnReset empty and ReadLocked/WriteLocked True. Core says an empty LockOnReset does not change those values on any reset, so returning False/False after the power cycle is not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: The prior Set left LockOnReset empty and ReadLocked/WriteLocked False. Core says an empty LockOnReset set leaves the locking state unchanged across resets, so False/False after the hardware reset is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: With LockOnReset empty, Core requires ReadLocked and WriteLocked to remain unchanged on any reset. They were False before the hardware reset, so the target True/True result is not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: WriteLockEnabled was set False, so Core says the write-locking feature is disabled and the WriteLocked value is ignored. Even though the power-cycle LockOnReset value can set WriteLocked True, disabled write locking should not block the target write; SUCCESS is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: WriteLockEnabled was False, making the WriteLocked column ignored for this range. The target write should not be rejected on the basis of the locking state, so INVALID_PARAMETER is not supported by the cited locking rules.

label: FAIL
rationale: Power Cycle in LockOnReset would set WriteLocked True, but the power cycle also aborts all open sessions. The trajectory does not start a new session after the power cycle before issuing the target Get, so a successful method response in the aborted session is not compliant.
